NAPA COI'NTY
ROBERT WESTMEYER, Couflty Counsel
MARGAREr L. WOODBURY, Chief Deputy

Tony Holzhauer, a commissioner on the Napa County Conservation, Development and
Planning Commission (the "ComLnission"), hereby requests a written opinion from
your office as to whether a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act
exists based upon the following facts:
Commissioner Holzhauer is currently employed as a real esLaEe agent for Up
Valley Associates, a real eslate firx0 locaEed at LL26 Adams Street, St. Helena,
CA 94574 (nup Valleyx). As a commissioned agent for Up Valley, Conmissioner
Holzhauer assists the firm in marketing ics listed properEies. Commissioner
Holzhauer currently has several listings in the unicorporated areas of Napa
County, but has earned no commissions in the past twelve months from the sale of
properties located in unincorporated Napa County. He may earn such commissions
in the future should a favorable opportunily present itself.
The Housing Elernent of the Napa County General Plan states that the provision of
affordable housing in the County is to be encouraged in connection with the
development of residential projects. The Commission is currently scheduled to
consider whether io recornmend to the Napa County Board of Supervisors (the
"Board") that an inclusionary housing ordinance be developed and adopted as part
of a comprehensive response to this aspect of the counEy's General plan. such a
reconnendation, if made, could include endorsement of a model ordinance
requiring some or all of the following: that a certain percentage of al1 new
residential units constructed in a project be "affordabre'! as that term is
defined in the ordinance; that a fee be paid to an affordable housing fund in
lieu of the actual conscruction of affordable units; or that construJtion of a
certain number of affordabre units take place off-site. 
rn addition, the
comrnission is llkely to recomnend the creation of additional sources of funding
for affordable housing through means such as assessment of rinkage fees fron
developers of agricultural, commercial and industrial projects blsed upon
proj ect - generated emplo]rnent, imposition of a business lilense tax or in"."".".
in documencary transfer or sales taxes. should cornmissioner Holzhauer ever


December 13, 1991

become involwed in the narketing of a property located in unincorporated Napa
County, such property would be subject to any ordinance ultimately adopted by
the Board.
The State Department of Housing and Community Development has required that some
form of inclusionary prograxo be referred to the Board by December 31, 199L.
Fallure to do so may reduce or elininate the ability of the County to qualify
for Comrnunity Development Block Grant funds. We would therefore very much
appreciate expedited handling of this request and the return of a written
opinlon to this office by December 26, 799I, if possible, so that a special
meeting of the Cornmission rnay be held on December 30, 1991. If you have any
quesEions as you conducE your review, please do not hesitate to contact either
ne (7O7 /253-452L) or Commissioner Holzhauer (7O7/963-L222 or 701 /963-9250).
Thank you for your altention to this natter.
Very truly yours,

sections.question: Whether a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act exists based upon Commissioner Tony Holzhauer's employment as a real estate agent for Up Valley Associates while serving on the Napa County Conservation, Development and Planning Commission, given that the Commission is scheduled to consider an inclusionary housing ordinance that could affect properties he may market in the future.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Commissioner Holzhauer have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act by serving on a planning commission while employed as a real estate agent, when the commission is considering an inclusionary housing ordinance that could affect properties he markets?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from County Counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No CONCLUSION or ANSWER section is present. Document contains detailed fact pattern and context but is seeking advice rather than providing it. Urgent deadline noted (December 26, 1991 response requested for December 30, 1991 meeting).

embedding.summary: Napa County Counsel requests a written opinion from the FPPC regarding whether a conflict of interest exists for Planning Commissioner Tony Holzhauer, who is employed as a real estate agent, in connection with the Commission's consideration of an inclusionary housing ordinance. The request seeks expedited handling with a response deadline of December 26, 1991.
